title: The Step Finance Autopsy: Why $27M in Audited Contracts Died From a Phishing Email
summary: Step Finance lost $27.3 million in 90 minutes after a phishing attack compromised an executive's laptop and extracted signing keys, despite the company's smart contracts being audited. The incident highlights a critical gap: code audits verify implementation security but not infrastructure security, key management, or endpoint protection—and private key compromises now account for 88% of crypto losses.
content:
On January 31, 2026, Step Finance — the self-proclaimed "front page of Solana" — lost $27.3 million in 90 minutes. Not from a flash loan. Not from a reentrancy bug. Not from an oracle manipulation.
From a compromised laptop.
Their smart contracts were audited. Their code was clean. Their bug bounty was live. And none of it mattered, because the attacker didn't need to hack the code — they just needed to phish an executive.
This isn't a post-mortem of Step Finance. It's a field manual for every DeFi team that thinks "we passed our audit" means "we're secure."
Here's the timeline, reconstructed from CertiK's on-chain analysis and Step Finance's own disclosures:
Step 1: Device Compromise
Step 2: Key Extraction
are the signer.
Step 3: Stake Authority Transfer
LEP1uHXcWbFEPwQgkeFzdhW2ykgZY6e9Dz8Yro6SdNu), then methodically unstaked 261,854 SOL from treasury and fee wallets.
Step 4: Extraction
Step 5: Discovery (Too Late)
hours after it completed. Their first public acknowledgment was a tweet asking cybersecurity firms to "slide into DMs." They eventually recovered $4.7 million through Token22 protections on Remora assets.
The STEP token crashed 93%. Step Finance and its subsidiaries (SolanaFloor, Remora Markets) shut down permanently.
Let's be clear about what smart contract audits do and don't cover:



What Audits Check
What Audits Don't Check




Reentrancy patterns
Who holds the private keys


Access control in code
Endpoint security on executive laptops


Integer overflow/underflow
Email phishing resilience


Oracle manipulation vectors
Off-hours monitoring coverage


Upgrade proxy safety
Multisig threshold adequacy


Business logic flaws
Key rotation policies



Step Finance had every box checked on the left column. Zero coverage on the right.
This isn't an anomaly. Chainalysis data shows private key compromises drove 88% of crypto losses in Q1 2025, and the pattern accelerated into 2026. The industry's security investment is overwhelmingly concentrated on smart co

zh.title: Step Finance尸检：为什么价值2700万美元的审计智能合约因钓鱼邮件而失效
zh.summary: Step Finance在执行官笔记本被钓鱼攻击入侵并提取签名密钥后，90分钟内损失了2730万美元，尽管该公司的智能合约已经过审计。这一事件凸显了一个关键漏洞：代码审计验证实现安全性，但不验证基础设施安全性、密钥管理或端点保护——私钥泄露现在占加密货币损失的88%。

fr.title: L'autopsie de Step Finance : Pourquoi 27 millions de dollars de contrats audités sont morts d'un email de phishing
fr.summary: Step Finance a perdu 27,3 millions de dollars en 90 minutes après qu'une attaque par phishing ait compromis l'ordinateur portable d'un cadre et extrait les clés de signature, malgré l'audit des contrats intelligents de l'entreprise. L'incident met en évidence une lacune critique : les audits de code vérifient la sécurité de la mise en œuvre, mais pas la sécurité de l'infrastructure, la gestion des clés ou la protection des points d'extrémité — et les compromissions de clés privées représentent maintenant 88 % des pertes de crypto-monnaies.

de.title: Die Step Finance Obduktion: Warum intelligente Verträge im Wert von 27 Millionen Dollar durch eine Phishing-E-Mail starben
de.summary: Step Finance verlor 27,3 Millionen Dollar in 90 Minuten, nachdem ein Phishing-Angriff den Laptop eines Executives kompromittierte und Signierschlüssel extrahierte, obwohl die Smart Contracts des Unternehmens geprüft worden waren. Der Vorfall hebt eine kritische Lücke hervor: Code-Audits überprüfen die Sicherheit der Implementierung, aber nicht die Infrastruktursicherheit, Schlüsselverwaltung oder Endpoint-Schutz — und private Schlüsselkompromisse machen nun 88% der Kryptoverluste aus.

es.title: La autopsia de Step Finance: Por qué 27 millones de dólares en contratos auditados murieron por un correo de phishing
es.summary: Step Finance perdió 27,3 millones de dólares en 90 minutos después de que un ataque de phishing comprometiera la computadora portátil de un ejecutivo y extrajera claves de firma, a pesar de que los contratos inteligentes de la empresa fueron auditados. El incidente destaca una brecha crítica: las auditorías de código verifican la seguridad de la implementación, pero no la seguridad de la infraestructura, la gestión de claves o la protección de puntos finales — y los compromisos de claves privadas ahora representan el 88% de las pérdidas de criptografía.

title: Calldata Injection: The $17M Vulnerability Pattern Hiding in Every DeFi Router
summary: Calldata injection vulnerabilities in DeFi routers and aggregators allowed attackers to steal $17 million from SwapNet and Aperture Finance by exploiting low-level calls with user-controlled data. The flaw occurs when contracts fail to validate call targets, enabling attackers to redirect user approvals to malicious addresses.

zh.title: Calldata注入：隐藏在每个DeFi路由器中的1700万美元漏洞模式
zh.summary: DeFi路由器和聚合器中的Calldata注入漏洞允许攻击者通过利用包含用户控制数据的低级调用从SwapNet和Aperture Finance窃取1700万美元。当合约未能验证调用目标时会发生此漏洞，使攻击者能够将用户批准重定向到恶意地址。

fr.title: Injection Calldata : Le modèle de vulnérabilité de 17 millions de dollars caché dans chaque routeur DeFi
fr.summary: Les vulnérabilités d'injection calldata dans les routeurs DeFi et les agrégateurs ont permis aux attaquants de voler 17 millions de dollars à SwapNet et Aperture Finance en exploitant des appels de bas niveau avec des données contrôlées par l'utilisateur. La faille survient lorsque les contrats ne valident pas les cibles d'appel, permettant aux attaquants de rediriger les approbations des utilisateurs vers des adresses malveillantes.

de.title: Calldata-Injektion: Das 17-Millionen-Dollar-Sicherheitslückenmuster, das in jedem DeFi-Router versteckt ist
de.summary: Calldata-Injektions-Anfälligkeit in DeFi-Routern und Aggregatoren ermöglichten es Angreifern, 17 Millionen Dollar von SwapNet und Aperture Finance zu stehlen, indem sie Low-Level-Aufrufe mit benutzergesteuerten Daten ausnutzten. Der Fehler tritt auf, wenn Verträge die Aufrufe nicht validieren, was es Angreifern ermöglicht, Benutzer-Genehmigungen auf bösartige Adressen umzuleiten.

es.title: Inyección Calldata: El patrón de vulnerabilidad de 17 millones de dólares oculto en cada enrutador DeFi
es.summary: Las vulnerabilidades de inyección de calldata en enrutadores DeFi y agregadores permitieron a los atacantes robar 17 millones de dólares de SwapNet y Aperture Finance explotando llamadas de bajo nivel con datos controlados por el usuario. La falla ocurre cuando los contratos no validan los destinos de llamada, permitiendo a los atacantes redirigir las aprobaciones del usuario a direcciones maliciosas.

title: Ask the community: building real-time audio in C++ — what would you add?
summary: Developer seeks community input on prioritizing features for Amplitron, an open-source C++17 guitar amp simulator with working DSP effects, tube modeling, and cabinet simulation. Requested features include additional effect types, MIDI support, multi-amp chains, better visualization, and VST/AU plugin versions.
content:
I’m actively developing Amplitron, a free open-source guitar amp simulator in C++17, and I'd love to get community input on the direction of the project.
The core is solid — we have DSP effects chains, tube amp modeling, and cabinet simulation working well. But I want to know what would make it more useful for the community.
Some ideas I’m considering:
More effects types (reverb, delay, modulation effects)
MIDI controller support
Multi-amp/split chains
Better visualization of the signal chain
Plugin version (VST/AU)
But I’m curious: what effects or features would YOU prioritize if you were building a guitar amp simulator? Are you a guitarist, game developer, or audio enthusiast? What would make this tool actually useful for your projects?
Feel free to check out the full implementation here: https://dev.to/sudipmondal2002/i-built-a-free-open-source-guitar-amp-simulator-in-c17-heres-how-1d86
Looking forward to hearing your ideas!

zh.title: 请求社区意见：在C++中构建实时音频——你会添加什么？
zh.summary: 开发人员寻求社区意见，以优先考虑Amplitron（一个开源C++17吉他放大器模拟器，具有工作DSP效果、管建模和箱体模拟）的功能。请求的功能包括额外的效果类型、MIDI支持、多放大器链、更好的可视化和VST/AU插件版本。

fr.title: Demander à la communauté : construire l'audio en temps réel en C++ — qu'ajouteriez-vous ?
fr.summary: Le développeur demande l'avis de la communauté sur la priorisation des fonctionnalités pour Amplitron, un simulateur d'amplificateur de guitare C++17 open-source avec des effets DSP, une modélisation de tube et une simulation de cabinet. Les fonctionnalités demandées incluent des types d'effets supplémentaires, la prise en charge MIDI, des chaînes multi-amplis, une meilleure visualisation et des versions de plugin VST/AU.

de.title: Die Community fragen: Echtzeit-Audio in C++ erstellen — was würden Sie hinzufügen?
de.summary: Entwickler sucht Community-Beiträge zur Priorisierung von Funktionen für Amplitron, einen Open-Source-C++17-Gitarrenverstärker-Simulator mit funktionierenden DSP-Effekten, Röhrenmodellierung und Gehäusesimulation. Angeforderte Funktionen umfassen zusätzliche Effekttypen, MIDI-Unterstützung, Multi-Amp-Ketten, bessere Visualisierung und VST/AU-Plugin-Versionen.

es.title: Preguntar a la comunidad: construir audio en tiempo real en C++ — ¿qué agregarías?
es.summary: El desarrollador busca aportes de la comunidad para priorizar características de Amplitron, un simulador de amplificador de guitarra C++17 de código abierto con efectos DSP, modelado de tubos y simulación de gabinete. Las características solicitadas incluyen tipos de efectos adicionales, soporte MIDI, cadenas multi-amplificador, mejor visualización y versiones de complementos VST/AU.

title: How to Rebuild Trust After a Leadership Failure: Communication Playbook
summary: Guide for leaders to rebuild damaged team trust after failure through sustained, honest communication paired with visible behavioral changes. Repair requires uncomfortable acknowledgment of what went wrong, specific commitments for change, and consistent follow-through on smaller promises to restore credibility.
content:
When You're the One Who Broke the Trust


You made a promise you didn't keep. You failed to advocate for your team when it mattered. You made a decision that hurt people and didn't own it fast enough. You were absent during a crisis. Whatever happened, your team's trust in you is damaged — and you can feel it in every interaction.
Damaged trust changes the physics of leadership. People stop sharing bad news. They comply instead of commit. They start job searching. The talent you worked hardest to develop becomes the talent most likely to leave, because high performers have options and they don't waste them on leaders they don't trust.
Trust repair isn't a single conversation. It's a sustained communication strategy that demonstrates through consistent action that you understand what went wrong and you've changed.
Subject: Addressing [specific situation] directly
Hi team, I need to address [what happened] directly. What I did/didn't do: [specific, honest account — no minimizing, no blame-shifting]. The impact: [what it cost the team — be specific about how it affected them, not how it affected you]. I was wrong. Not 'mistakes were made' — I made this mistake, and I own it. Here's what I'm doing to make sure it doesn't happen again: [specific changes — not vague commitments, but concrete actions]. I know trust takes time to rebuild. I'm committed to that process. [Your name]
This email must be uncomfortably honest. If you're comfortable sending it, you probably haven't gone deep enough. The discomfort IS the signal that you're being genuine.
Words started the repair. Actions complete it. During this phase, your behavior needs to be visibly different from the behavior that broke trust.
If you failed to advocate: visibly advocate in the next opportunity and tell your team you did it. If you broke a promise: make a smaller promise and keep it. Then another. Then another. If you were absent in crisis: be the first to respond to the next one, even if it's small.
Over-commu

zh.title: 如何在领导失败后重建信任：沟通手册
zh.summary: 领导者通过持续真诚的沟通和可见的行为改变来重建团队信任的指南。修复需要不舒适地承认问题所在、做出具体的改变承诺，并一致地遵守小承诺以恢复信誉。

fr.title: Comment Reconstruire la Confiance Après un Échec de Leadership : Playbook de Communication
fr.summary: Guide pour les leaders reconstruire la confiance d'équipe endommagée après un échec par une communication honnête soutenue associée à des changements comportementaux visibles. La réparation nécessite une reconnaissance inconfortable de ce qui s'est mal passé, des engagements spécifiques pour le changement, et un suivi cohérent des promesses mineures pour restaurer la crédibilité.

de.title: Vertrauen nach einem Führungsversagen wiederherstellen: Kommunikations-Playbook
de.summary: Leitfaden für Führungskräfte, um beschädigtes Teamvertrauen nach Versagen durch anhaltende, ehrliche Kommunikation gepaart mit sichtbaren Verhaltensänderungen wiederherzustellen. Reparatur erfordert unbequemes Eingeständnis, was schief gelaufen ist, spezifische Verpflichtungen zur Veränderung und konsequente Umsetzung kleinerer Versprechen, um Glaubwürdigkeit wiederherzustellen.

es.title: Cómo Reconstruir la Confianza Después de un Fracaso de Liderazgo: Manual de Comunicación
es.summary: Guía para que los líderes reconstruyan la confianza del equipo dañada después del fracaso a través de una comunicación honesta sostenida acompañada de cambios de comportamiento visibles. La reparación requiere un incómodo reconocimiento de lo que salió mal, compromisos específicos para el cambio, y el cumplimiento consistente de promesas menores para restaurar la credibilidad.

title: Documenting Emotional Labor at Work: How to Make Invisible Work Visible
summary: Framework for making invisible emotional labor—mentoring, conflict resolution, onboarding—visible in performance reviews by documenting time spent and translating it into business language. Recognizing this work is critical for compensation and retention since high performers often leave when effort goes unacknowledged.
content:
The Work Nobody Sees


You're the person who remembers everyone's birthdays. You're the one who notices when a colleague is struggling and checks in. You organize the team events. You mediate conflicts. You onboard new hires by answering their 'dumb questions' for the first three months. You do all of this on top of your actual job.
This is emotional labor — the invisible work of maintaining relationships, managing feelings, and keeping the human machinery of a team running. It's real work. It takes real time. And it's almost never acknowledged in performance reviews, promotions, or compensation because it's categorized as 'being nice' rather than 'contributing value.'
The first step to changing this: making the invisible visible. You can't be compensated for work your manager doesn't know you're doing.
Keep a simple log. For one month, note every instance of emotional labor: mentoring conversations, conflict mediation, onboarding support, event coordination, morale management, team culture maintenance. Include approximate time spent.
Translate it into business language. 'Helped Sarah feel better after the client meeting' → 'Provided post-meeting coaching to junior team member, improving client-facing skills and retention risk.' 'Planned the holiday party' → 'Coordinated team engagement event that improved Q4 morale scores.'
The translation isn't dishonest. It's reframing work in the language that organizations recognize as valuable. The coaching was real. The morale impact was real. You're just giving it the vocabulary it deserves.
Subject: Contributions beyond core responsibilities
Hi [Manager], As I prepare for my review, I want to highlight contributions that fall outside my formal job description but significantly impact the team: [Team culture]: organized [X events], coordinated [Y], maintained [Z tradition]. Approximate time: [hours/month]. [Mentorship]: provided ongoing coaching to [number] team members on [topics]. [Conflict resolution]: mediated [number] i

zh.title: 记录工作中的情感劳动：如何让无形工作可见
zh.summary: 通过记录花费的时间并将其转化为商业语言，在绩效评估中使看不见的情感劳动（指导、冲突解决、入职）可见的框架。认识到这项工作对薪酬和留任至关重要，因为当努力得不到认可时，高绩效员工往往会离职。

fr.title: Documenter le Travail Émotionnel au Travail : Comment Rendre le Travail Invisible Visible
fr.summary: Cadre pour rendre le travail émotionnel invisible—mentorat, résolution de conflits, intégration—visible dans les évaluations de performance en documentant le temps consacré et en le traduisant en langage commercial. Reconnaître ce travail est essentiel pour la rémunération et la rétention puisque les meilleurs éléments partent souvent lorsque l'effort n'est pas reconnu.

de.title: Emotionale Arbeit bei der Arbeit dokumentieren: Wie man unsichtbare Arbeit sichtbar macht
de.summary: Rahmenwerk zur Sichtbarmachung unsichtbarer emotionaler Arbeit—Mentoring, Konfliktlösung, Onboarding—in Leistungsbewertungen durch Dokumentation aufgewendeter Zeit und deren Übersetzung in Geschäftssprache. Diese Arbeit anzuerkennen ist entscheidend für Vergütung und Mitarbeiterbindung, da Top-Performer oft gehen, wenn ihre Bemühungen nicht anerkannt werden.

es.title: Documentar el Trabajo Emocional en el Trabajo: Cómo Hacer Visible el Trabajo Invisible
es.summary: Marco para hacer visible el trabajo emocional invisible—mentoría, resolución de conflictos, incorporación—en revisiones de desempeño documentando el tiempo dedicado y traduciéndolo al lenguaje comercial. Reconocer este trabajo es crítico para la compensación y retención ya que los empleados de alto rendimiento a menudo se van cuando el esfuerzo no es reconocido.

title: The Surprising Economics of Free APIs: Why Companies Give Away Data Worth Millions
summary: Companies offer free APIs for valuable services because user data enriches their paid products; VirusTotal, Shodan, GitHub, and Reddit leverage free API users to improve threat detection, search maps, code analysis, and advertising. Free APIs create network effects that actually incentivize reliability and good documentation.
content:
Last month, I discovered something strange while building a security tool.
VirusTotal lets you scan files for malware — for free. They analyze each file against 70+ antivirus engines. The compute cost per scan must be enormous. Yet their API is free for up to 500 requests per day.
Why would a company give that away?
I went down a rabbit hole and found a pattern that explains why some of the most valuable data on the internet is available through free APIs.
Here are real examples:



Company
Free API
What They Sell




VirusTotal (Google)
500 scans/day
Enterprise threat intelligence


Shodan
1 query/sec
Bulk data + monitoring


Have I Been Pwned
Email breach checks
Domain-wide monitoring


GitHub
Full repo/user data
Copilot, Actions, Enterprise


Reddit
Public JSON endpoints
Advertising platform



The free API is not the product. You are the product — or rather, your data enriches their product.
Every time you scan a file on VirusTotal, you're training their malware detection. Every Shodan query helps them map the internet. Every HIBP check validates their breach database.
This is actually great news. These companies are incentivized to keep their APIs free and reliable because:
More users = better data — network effects work in your favor
API stability matters — breaking changes lose them data contributors
Documentation stays good — they want you to integrate, not give up
import requests

# Check if an email has been in a data breach
def check_breach(email):
    resp = requests.get(
        f"https://haveibeenpwned.com/api/v3/breachedaccount/{email}",
        headers={"User-Agent": "SecurityAudit/1.0"}
    )
    return resp.json() if resp.status_code == 200 else []

# Get Reddit posts without API key
def get_reddit_posts(subreddit, sort="hot", limit=100):
    url = f"https://www.reddit.com/r/{subreddit}/{sort}.json?limit={limit}"
    resp = requests.get(url, headers={"User-Agent": "DataResearch/1.0"})
    return [p["data"] for p in resp.json()["data"]["children"]]

zh.title: 免费API的惊人经济学：为什么公司免费提供价值数百万的数据
zh.summary: 公司提供有价值服务的免费API，因为用户数据丰富他们的付费产品；VirusTotal、Shodan、GitHub和Reddit利用免费API用户来改进威胁检测、搜索地图、代码分析和广告。免费API创造了网络效应，实际上鼓励可靠性和良好的文档记录。

fr.title: L'Économie Surprenante des API Gratuites : Pourquoi les Entreprises Donnent des Données Valant des Millions
fr.summary: Les entreprises proposent des API gratuites pour des services précieux car les données utilisateur enrichissent leurs produits payants ; VirusTotal, Shodan, GitHub et Reddit exploitent les utilisateurs d'API gratuites pour améliorer la détection des menaces, les cartes de recherche, l'analyse de code et la publicité. Les API gratuites créent des effets de réseau qui encouragent réellement la fiabilité et une bonne documentation.

de.title: Die überraschende Ökonomie kostenloser APIs: Warum Unternehmen Daten im Wert von Millionen verschenken
de.summary: Unternehmen bieten kostenlose APIs für wertvolle Dienste an, weil Benutzerdaten ihre bezahlten Produkte bereichern; VirusTotal, Shodan, GitHub und Reddit nutzen kostenlose API-Benutzer zur Verbesserung der Bedrohungserkennung, Suchkarten, Codeanalyse und Werbung. Kostenlose APIs erzeugen Netzwerkeffekte, die tatsächlich Zuverlässigkeit und gute Dokumentation fördern.

es.title: La Sorprendente Economía de las API Gratuitas: Por Qué las Empresas Regalan Datos Valorados en Millones
es.summary: Las empresas ofrecen API gratuitas para servicios valiosos porque los datos de usuarios enriquecen sus productos pagados; VirusTotal, Shodan, GitHub y Reddit aprovechan a los usuarios de API gratuitas para mejorar la detección de amenazas, mapas de búsqueda, análisis de código y publicidad. Las API gratuitas crean efectos de red que realmente incentivan la confiabilidad y la buena documentación.

title: Writing Neurodivergent-Friendly Work Emails: A Guide for Managers and Teams
summary: Writing clear, structured emails with explicit language, labeled sections, and direct action items helps neurodivergent employees (ADHD, autism, dyslexia) while improving communication for everyone. Practices like replacing idioms, breaking text with headers, and stating deadlines specifically reduce cognitive load across the entire team.
content:
The Hidden Communication Barrier


An estimated 15-20% of the workforce is neurodivergent — ADHD, autism spectrum, dyslexia, dyscalculia, dyspraxia, and other neurological differences. Most workplace communication was never designed for how these brains process information.
The result: neurodivergent employees spend significant cognitive energy decoding communication that neurotypical colleagues process automatically. Vague instructions, implicit expectations, ambiguous tone, and unstructured information create a tax that's invisible to the majority but exhausting for the minority.
The good news: communication practices that help neurodivergent team members help everyone. Clear, structured, explicit communication isn't an accommodation — it's just better communication.
Use clear subject lines that indicate action: 'ACTION NEEDED: Review proposal by Friday' vs. 'Quick question.' The first tells the reader exactly what's needed and when. The second tells them nothing until they read the entire email.
Break emails into labeled sections: CONTEXT (why this matters), REQUEST (what you need), DEADLINE (when you need it), NEXT STEPS (what happens after). This structure helps ADHD brains prioritize, autistic brains parse expectations, and honestly, every brain process information more efficiently.
One email, one topic. Emails that cover three unrelated topics force the reader to track multiple threads and remember multiple actions. Send separate emails for separate topics. It feels like more email but it's actually less cognitive load.
Say what you mean, directly. 'It might be worth considering whether we should perhaps look at the timeline' → 'The timeline needs to change. Here's what I'm proposing.' The first sentence requires five layers of inference. The second requires zero.
Make deadlines specific. 'Soon' means different things to different brains. 'By end of day' is ambiguous across time zones. 'By 5 PM EST on March 28' is clear to everyone.
State the emotional contex

zh.title: 撰写神经多样性友好的工作邮件：经理和团队指南
zh.summary: 使用明确的语言、有标签的部分和直接的行动项目撰写清晰、结构化的电子邮件，可以帮助神经多样性员工（ADHD、自闭症、阅读障碍），同时改善所有人的沟通。用习语替换、用标题分割文本和具体说明截止日期等做法可以减少整个团队的认知负荷。

fr.title: Rédiger des e-mails professionnels accessibles aux neurodivergents : Un guide pour les responsables et les équipes
fr.summary: Écrire des e-mails clairs et structurés avec un langage explicite, des sections étiquetées et des éléments d'action directs aide les employés neurodivergents (TDAH, autisme, dyslexie) tout en améliorant la communication pour tous. Les pratiques telles que remplacer les idiomes, diviser le texte avec des en-têtes et énoncer clairement les délais réduisent la charge cognitive de toute l'équipe.

de.title: Neurodivergenz-freundliche Arbeitsemails schreiben: Ein Leitfaden für Manager und Teams
de.summary: Das Schreiben von klaren, strukturierten E-Mails mit expliziter Sprache, gekennzeichneten Abschnitten und direkten Aktionselementen hilft neurodivergenten Mitarbeitern (ADHS, Autismus, Legasthenie), während die Kommunikation für alle verbessert wird. Praktiken wie das Ersetzen von Redewendungen, das Aufteilen von Text mit Überschriften und das konkrete Angeben von Fristen reduzieren die kognitive Belastung im gesamten Team.

es.title: Cómo escribir correos electrónicos de trabajo accesibles para neurodivergentes: Una guía para gerentes y equipos
es.summary: Escribir correos electrónicos claros y estructurados con lenguaje explícito, secciones etiquetadas y elementos de acción directos ayuda a los empleados neurodivergentes (TDAH, autismo, dislexia) mientras mejora la comunicación para todos. Las prácticas como reemplazar modismos, dividir el texto con encabezados y establecer plazos específicamente reducen la carga cognitiva en todo el equipo.

title: Day 18 – Agentic AI For Software Development
summary: Development agents reduce cycle time and improve code quality by owning multi-file tasks end-to-end with goal-driven loops that run tests, analyze failures, and iterate—mirroring disciplined human engineers. Success depends on rich repo context, machine-verifiable feedback, and proper scoping rather than naive code generation.
content:
Executive Summary


Software development is one of the highest-ROI domains for agentic AI, not because agents write better code than humans, but because they can own well-scoped engineering workflows end to end.
When designed correctly, development agents can:
reduce cycle time from task → PR

improve test coverage and code quality

offload repetitive cognitive load from senior engineers



When designed poorly, they:
generate noisy diffs

break abstractions

erode trust in the codebase



This chapter focuses on production-grade usage, not demos.
Unlike many business domains, software development already has:
explicit goals (tickets, issues, PRs)

machine-verifiable feedback (tests, CI)

rich tool surfaces (repos, linters, debuggers)



This makes it ideal for goal-driven autonomous loops.
However, development is also:
stateful

highly contextual

full of implicit conventions



Which is why naïve agent designs fail.



Dimension
Code Assistants
Dev Agents




Scope
Single file / function
Multi-file, repo-wide


Awareness
Local context
System + repo context


Feedback loop
None
Tests, CI, errors


Autonomy
Suggestive
Goal-driven


Output
Snippets
Working PRs



Agents operate at the task level, not the keystroke level.
        Ticket / Task
              ↓
     Requirement Interpreter
              ↓
     Repo Exploration Agent
              ↓
        Planning Module
              ↓
     ┌──────── Execution Loop ────────┐
     │   Edit Code → Run Tests → Fix  │
     └───────────────────────────────┘
              ↓
        Validation Gate
              ↓
        Pull Request

Key observation:
The agent does not ship code — it earns the right to propose it.
while not success:
    understand_task()
    identify_relevant_files()
    plan_changes()
    apply_changes()
    run_tests()
    analyze_failures()
    if iteration_limit_reached:
        stop_and_report()

This mirrors a disciplined human engineer.
“Introduce rate limiting on the login endpoint without changing

zh.title: 第18天 – 用于软件开发的代理型AI
zh.summary: 开发代理通过拥有端到端的多文件任务，使用目标驱动的循环来运行测试、分析失败和迭代，从而减少周期时间并提高代码质量——模仿纪律严明的人类工程师。成功取决于丰富的代码库上下文、机器可验证的反馈和适当的范围界定，而不是天真的代码生成。

fr.title: Jour 18 – L'IA générative pour le développement logiciel
fr.summary: Les agents de développement réduisent le temps de cycle et améliorent la qualité du code en possédant des tâches multi-fichiers de bout en bout avec des boucles orientées vers les objectifs qui exécutent des tests, analysent les défaillances et itèrent—en imitant les ingénieurs humains disciplinés. Le succès dépend du contexte riche du dépôt, des retours vérifiables par machine et d'une portée appropriée plutôt que de la génération naïve de code.

de.title: Tag 18 – Agentic AI für Softwareentwicklung
de.summary: Entwicklungsagenten reduzieren die Zykluszeit und verbessern die Codequalität, indem sie End-to-End-Multi-File-Aufgaben mit zielgesteuerten Schleifen übernehmen, die Tests ausführen, Fehler analysieren und iterieren – nach dem Vorbild disziplinierter menschlicher Ingenieure. Der Erfolg hängt von reichhaltigem Repo-Kontext, maschinenverifizierbarem Feedback und angemessener Bereichsfestlegung ab, nicht von naiver Codegenerierung.

es.title: Día 18 – IA Agencial para Desarrollo de Software
es.summary: Los agentes de desarrollo reducen el tiempo de ciclo y mejoran la calidad del código al ser dueños de tareas de múltiples archivos de extremo a extremo con bucles orientados a objetivos que ejecutan pruebas, analizan fallos e iteran, espejando ingenieros humanos disciplinados. El éxito depende del contexto rico del repositorio, retroalimentación verificable por máquina y un alcance apropiado en lugar de generación de código ingenua.

title: Day 19 – Customer Support Agents (tickets Resolution)
summary: Customer support agents can triage issues, reason over historical context, and coordinate tools across the ticket lifecycle while reducing resolution time. The critical difference from chatbots is that agents own outcomes rather than answer individual messages, requiring deliberately conservative design since user frustration and incorrect actions can damage brand trust.
content:
Executive Summary


Customer support is one of the most economically impactful applications of agentic AI.
Not because agents can "chat politely" 🙂 — but because they can:
triage issues at scale

reason over historical context

coordinate tools and workflows

reduce resolution time without degrading trust



When done well, support agents:
lower operational costs 💰

improve first-contact resolution 📈

free human agents for high-empathy cases ❤️



When done poorly, they:
frustrate users 😤

hallucinate solutions

damage brand trust



This chapter focuses on end-to-end ticket resolution systems, not chatbots.
Support workflows naturally align with agentic systems because they involve:
ambiguous problem statements ❓

multi-step investigation 🔍

tool-heavy resolution paths 🔧

judgment calls ⚖️



But they are dangerous because:
users are already frustrated 😠

incorrect actions can cause real harm 🚨

trust is fragile



Agentic support systems must be deliberately conservative.



Dimension
Chatbots
Support Agents




Scope
Single response
Full ticket lifecycle


Context
Current message
User + account + history


Tools
None / limited
CRM, logs, billing, KB


Autonomy
Reactive
Goal-driven


Risk
Low
High



A chatbot answers questions.

A support agent owns outcomes.
        User Ticket 🎫
              ↓
     Intent & Severity Classifier
              ↓
      Context Aggregator
   (User, Account, History)
              ↓
      Diagnosis & Planning
              ↓
     ┌────── Resolution Loop ──────┐
     │  Query Tools → Observe → Decide │
     └───────────────────────────────┘
              ↓
     Action / Recommendation Engine
              ↓
        Validation Gate 🚦
              ↓
        User Response ✉️

Key principle:
Agents recommend actions; systems execute them.
understand_issue()
gather_context()
hypothesize_cause()
validate_with_tools()
select_resolution()
confirm_safety()
respond_or_escalate()

This mirrors how senior support engineers operate.
Hi

zh.title: 第19天 – 客户支持代理（工单解决）
zh.summary: 客户支持代理可以进行问题分类、基于历史背景进行推理，并在工单生命周期中协调工具，同时减少解决时间。与聊天机器人的关键区别在于，代理拥有结果而不是回答单个消息，这要求刻意采用保守的设计，因为用户沮丧和不正确的操作可能会损害品牌信任。

fr.title: Jour 19 – Agents de support client (résolution des tickets)
fr.summary: Les agents de support client peuvent trier les problèmes, raisonner sur le contexte historique et coordonner les outils tout au long du cycle de vie du ticket tout en réduisant le temps de résolution. La différence critique avec les chatbots est que les agents possèdent les résultats plutôt que de répondre à des messages individuels, ce qui nécessite une conception délibérément conservatrice car la frustration de l'utilisateur et les actions incorrectes peuvent endommager la confiance dans la marque.

de.title: Tag 19 – Customer-Support-Agenten (Ticket-Lösung)
de.summary: Customer-Support-Agenten können Probleme klassifizieren, über historische Kontexte nachdenken und Tools über den Ticket-Lebenszyklus hinweg koordinieren und gleichzeitig die Lösungszeit verkürzen. Der entscheidende Unterschied zu Chatbots besteht darin, dass Agenten Ergebnisse besitzen, anstatt auf einzelne Nachrichten zu antworten, was ein bewusst konservatives Design erfordert, da Benutzerfrustration und falsche Maßnahmen das Markenvertrauen beschädigen können.

es.title: Día 19 – Agentes de soporte al cliente (resolución de tickets)
es.summary: Los agentes de soporte al cliente pueden clasificar problemas, razonar sobre el contexto histórico y coordinar herramientas en todo el ciclo de vida del ticket mientras se reduce el tiempo de resolución. La diferencia crítica con los chatbots es que los agentes son dueños de los resultados en lugar de responder a mensajes individuales, lo que requiere un diseño deliberadamente conservador, ya que la frustración del usuario y las acciones incorrectas pueden dañar la confianza de la marca.

title: Inclusive Communication at Work: How to Write Emails Everyone Can Engage With
summary: Workplace emails become more inclusive by avoiding idioms, stating actions clearly, front-loading information, and writing for literal readers. These practices—which help neurodivergent and non-native English speakers—actually improve clarity and efficiency for all team members regardless of communication style or background.
content:
Why 'Normal' Communication Excludes People


Standard workplace communication was designed by and for a specific demographic. The norms — indirect requests, implied context, cultural idioms, and assumption-heavy shorthand — work well for people who share the dominant culture. For everyone else, they create invisible barriers.
This isn't about political correctness. It's about effectiveness. If 30% of your team is interpreting your emails differently than you intended — because of cultural background, language proficiency, neurodivergence, or communication style — you're operating at 70% efficiency. Inclusive communication isn't a nice-to-have. It's a performance optimization.
The changes are small. The impact is significant.
Replace idioms with direct language. 'Let's circle back' → 'Let's discuss this again on Thursday.' 'Low-hanging fruit' → 'Tasks we can complete quickly.' 'Move the needle' → 'Make measurable progress.' Idioms assume shared cultural knowledge. Direct language assumes nothing.
State the action clearly. 'It would be great if someone could look into the data issue' → '[Name], please investigate the data discrepancy and report back by Friday.' The first version might be politely ambiguous to a native English speaker. To a non-native speaker, it's genuinely ambiguous about who should act and when.
Write for your most literal reader. If someone could misinterpret your email by reading it exactly as written, rewrite it. Sarcasm, understatement, and British-style indirection ('I'm not entirely sure that's the best approach' meaning 'that's wrong') don't survive translation.
Use headers and bullet points for emails longer than three paragraphs. Dense text blocks are harder for everyone to parse — and significantly harder for people with dyslexia, ADHD, or anyone reading in their second language.
Front-load the action item. If your email requires someone to do something, put it in the first two sentences. Don't bury it in paragraph four after context they

zh.title: 职场包容性沟通：如何撰写所有人都能参与的电子邮件
zh.summary: 职场电子邮件通过避免习语、清晰陈述行动、前置信息和针对字面理解者写作而变得更具包容性。这些做法——有助于神经发散者和非英语使用者——实际上能提高所有团队成员的清晰度和效率，无论其沟通风格或背景如何。

fr.title: Communication inclusive au travail : comment rédiger des e-mails avec lesquels tout le monde peut s'engager
fr.summary: Les e-mails professionnels deviennent plus inclusifs en évitant les idiomes, en énonçant clairement les actions, en mettant l'information en avant et en écrivant pour les lecteurs littéraux. Ces pratiques—qui aident les personnes neurodivergentes et les non-anglophones—améliorent en réalité la clarté et l'efficacité pour tous les membres de l'équipe, quel que soit leur style de communication ou leur origine.

de.title: Inklusive Kommunikation bei der Arbeit: Wie man E-Mails schreibt, an denen alle teilnehmen können
de.summary: Geschäfts-E-Mails werden durch das Vermeiden von Idiomen, das klare Angeben von Aktionen, das Voranstellen von Informationen und das Schreiben für buchstäbliche Leser inklusiver. Diese Praktiken – die neurodivergenten und nicht-englischen Sprechern helfen – verbessern tatsächlich die Klarheit und Effizienz für alle Teammitglieder, unabhängig von ihrem Kommunikationsstil oder Hintergrund.

es.title: Comunicación inclusiva en el trabajo: Cómo escribir correos electrónicos con los que todos puedan participar
es.summary: Los correos electrónicos del lugar de trabajo se vuelven más inclusivos al evitar modismos, establecer acciones con claridad, poner la información en primer lugar y escribir para lectores literales. Estas prácticas—que ayudan a personas neurodivergentes y hablantes no nativos de inglés—mejoran la claridad y eficiencia para todos los miembros del equipo, independientemente de su estilo de comunicación o trasfondo.

title: Coding agents as runtime systems
summary: Coding AI is shifting from local code completion to full agent runtimes that manage state, call tools, branch on conditions, and learn from traces. This architectural change means reliability now depends on trace capture, control flow, and evaluation against task outcomes—making coding AI an infrastructure problem rather than a language model problem.
content:
One of the most technically interesting AI and coding stories from the last couple of weeks is the continued shift from code completion to full agent runtimes. OpenAI’s recent platform documentation emphasizes agent workflows with tools, logic nodes, trace grading, datasets, and the Agents SDK, which is a very different abstraction from the old “generate a function from a prompt” model. The center of gravity is moving upward, from token prediction to orchestration.  ￼
That matters because the hard part of serious coding is rarely local syntax. It is state, tool use, evaluation, and recovery after failure. Once an agent can call tools, inspect outputs, branch on conditions, and feed traces back into evaluation loops, the software problem starts to look less like autocomplete and more like a distributed runtime with an LLM inside it. In that architecture, prompts matter less than execution semantics.  ￼
The deeper point is that coding AI is becoming an infrastructure problem. Reliability now depends on trace capture, reproducible tool calls, control flow, and evaluation against task level outcomes, not just benchmark accuracy on isolated code snippets. That is a much more technical and much more interesting phase of the field.  ￼
This is why the story feels important. The next serious gains in AI coding may come not from slightly better code generation, but from better agent runtimes around the model.  ￼
Sources
https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/agent-builder
https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/agents-sdk
https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/trace-grading

zh.title: 代码代理作为运行时系统
zh.summary: 编程AI正在从本地代码完成转变为管理状态、调用工具、条件分支和从跟踪中学习的完整代理运行时。这一架构变化意味着可靠性现在取决于跟踪捕获、控制流和针对任务结果的评估——使编程AI成为基础设施问题而非语言模型问题。

fr.title: Les agents de codage comme systèmes d'exécution
fr.summary: L'IA de codage évolue de la complétion de code locale vers des runtimes d'agent complets qui gèrent l'état, appellent des outils, se ramifient sur des conditions et apprennent à partir de traces. Ce changement architectural signifie que la fiabilité dépend maintenant de la capture des traces, du flux de contrôle et de l'évaluation par rapport aux résultats des tâches—transformant l'IA de codage en problème d'infrastructure plutôt qu'en problème de modèle de langage.

de.title: Kodierungs-Agenten als Laufzeitsysteme
de.summary: Die Kodierungs-KI wechselt von lokaler Codevervollständigung zu vollständigen Agenten-Laufzeiten, die Zustand verwalten, Tools aufrufen, unter Bedingungen verzweigen und aus Traces lernen. Diese architektonische Änderung bedeutet, dass die Zuverlässigkeit nun von der Erfassung von Traces, dem Kontrollflusss und der Bewertung gegenüber Aufgabenergebnissen abhängt—was Kodierungs-KI zu einem Infrastrukturproblem statt zu einem Sprachmodellproblem macht.

es.title: Agentes de codificación como sistemas de tiempo de ejecución
es.summary: La IA de codificación está cambiando de la finalización de código local a tiempos de ejecución de agentes completos que administran estado, llaman herramientas, se ramifican en condiciones y aprenden de rastros. Este cambio arquitectónico significa que la confiabilidad ahora depende de la captura de rastros, el flujo de control y la evaluación contra resultados de tareas—haciendo que la IA de codificación sea un problema de infraestructura en lugar de un problema de modelo de lenguaje.

title: Building Psychological Safety Through Team Communication: A Manager's Playbook
summary: Psychological safety—where team members can take interpersonal risks without fear—is the single strongest predictor of team effectiveness, more important than talent or resources. Leaders build it through communication: responding to mistakes without punishment, rewarding questions, normalizing uncertainty, and celebrating useful failures.
content:
What Psychological Safety Actually Means


Psychological safety isn't about being nice. It's not about avoiding hard conversations or lowering standards. It's about creating an environment where people can take interpersonal risks — asking questions, admitting mistakes, challenging ideas — without fear of punishment or humiliation.
Google's Project Aristotle found it was the single most important factor in team effectiveness. More important than individual talent, resources, or strategy. Teams where people feel safe to be wrong outperform teams of brilliant people who are afraid to speak up.
And here's the problem: psychological safety is built or destroyed primarily through communication. Every email, every Slack message, every meeting response from a leader either reinforces safety or erodes it. You're always signaling, whether you mean to or not.
When someone admits a mistake: 'Thanks for flagging this early. That takes courage. Let's figure out the fix together.' NOT 'How did this happen?' The first response ensures they'll flag problems in the future. The second ensures they'll hide them.
When someone asks a 'dumb' question: answer it directly and completely. Never 'We covered this already' or 'That's in the docs.' The moment someone feels stupid for asking a question, they stop asking questions. And the questions they stop asking are the ones that prevent disasters.
When someone disagrees with you publicly: 'That's a good challenge. Tell me more about your thinking.' NOT 'I appreciate the feedback, but...' The word 'but' after appreciation cancels everything before it. Everyone in the room learns: disagreeing with the boss gets a polite shutdown.
Normalize 'I don't know.' Send an email that includes it: 'I'm not sure about the best approach here. I'd like to hear your perspectives before I form an opinion.' When the leader models not-knowing, the team gains permission to not-know too.
Celebrate useful failures. Monthly email to the team: 'Failure of the month:

zh.title: 通过团队沟通建立心理安全：管理者手册
zh.summary: 心理安全——团队成员可以在没有恐惧的情况下承担人际风险的地方——是团队有效性的最强单一预测因素，比人才或资源更重要。领导者通过沟通建立它：对错误做出回应而不惩罚、奖励问题、规范化不确定性和庆祝有用的失败。

fr.title: Construire la sécurité psychologique par la communication d'équipe : Un guide du gestionnaire
fr.summary: La sécurité psychologique—où les membres de l'équipe peuvent prendre des risques interpersonnels sans crainte—est le plus fort prédicteur unique de l'efficacité de l'équipe, plus important que le talent ou les ressources. Les leaders la construisent par la communication : répondre aux erreurs sans punition, récompenser les questions, normaliser l'incertitude et célébrer les échecs utiles.

de.title: Psychologische Sicherheit durch Teamkommunikation aufbauen: Ein Leitfaden für Manager
de.summary: Psychologische Sicherheit—wo Teammitglieder zwischenmenschliche Risiken ohne Angst eingehen können—ist der stärkste einzelne Prädiktor für Teameffektivität, wichtiger als Talent oder Ressourcen. Führungskräfte bauen sie durch Kommunikation auf: auf Fehler ohne Bestrafung reagieren, Fragen belohnen, Unsicherheit normalisieren und nützliche Fehler feiern.

es.title: Construir seguridad psicológica a través de la comunicación del equipo: Una guía para gerentes
es.summary: La seguridad psicológica—donde los miembros del equipo pueden asumir riesgos interpersonales sin miedo—es el predictor único más fuerte de la efectividad del equipo, más importante que el talento o los recursos. Los líderes la construyen a través de la comunicación: responder a errores sin castigo, recompensar preguntas, normalizar la incertidumbre y celebrar fracasos útiles.
